---
title: "seo report"
author: "Sujashree Roy"
date: "2026-05-27"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)

# Load data
df <- read_excel("~/Desktop/Company Analysis Kolkata/Kolkata SEO.xlsx")
# Clean column names
colnames(df) <- c("Company", "Clients", "Industries", "Focus", "Scale")
head(df)
# Split industries into rows
industry_df <- df %>%
  separate_rows(Industries, sep = ",\\s*")

# Clean industry names
industry_df$Industries <- str_trim(industry_df$Industries)
industry_count <- industry_df %>%
  count(Industries, sort = TRUE)

#plot the first 10 industries
industry_count_top10 <- industry_count %>%
  top_n(10, n)
ggplot(industry_count_top10, aes(x = reorder(Industries, n), y = n)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "pink") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Industries Served by SEO Companies in Kolkata",
       x = "Industry",
       y = "Number of Companies") +
  theme_minimal()

#SHOW the lowest crowded industries 
industry_count_bottom10 <- industry_count %>%
  top_n(-10, n)
print(industry_count_bottom10)
```



```{r}
#Plot clients in a bar chart
library(igraph)
# Split clients
client_df <- df %>%
  separate_rows(Clients, sep = ",\\s*")

client_df$Clients <- str_trim(client_df$Clients)

# Count most common clients
top_clients <- client_df %>%
  count(Clients, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)

# Keep only top 10 clients
filtered_df <- client_df %>%
  filter(Clients %in% top_clients$Clients)

# Create edges
edges <- filtered_df[, c("Company", "Clients")]

# Build graph
g <- graph_from_data_frame(edges)

# Plot cleaner graph
plot(
  g,
  layout = layout_with_fr,
  vertex.size = 18,
  vertex.label.cex = 0.7,
  vertex.color = ifelse(V(g)$name %in% top_clients$Clients,
                        "tomato",
                        "skyblue"),
  edge.arrow.size = 0.3,
  main = "Top 10 Shared Clients Network in SEO"
)
```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

# Create opportunity score
# -------------------------
# Competition Density
# -------------------------
# Split focus/services into separate rows
focus_df <- df %>%
  separate_rows(Focus, sep = ",\\s*")

# Clean spaces
focus_df$Focus <- str_trim(focus_df$Focus)
competition <- focus_df %>%
  count(Focus, sort = TRUE)

colnames(competition) <- c("Service", "Competition")

# -------------------------
# Market Demand
# -------------------------

demand <- focus_df %>%
  group_by(Focus) %>%
  summarise(
    Demand = sum(
      case_when(
        Scale == "Enterprise" ~ 3,
        Scale == "Mid" ~ 2,
        TRUE ~ 1
      )
    )
  )

# -------------------------
# Merge into Opportunity DF
# -------------------------

opportunity <- merge(
  competition,
  demand,
  by.x = "Service",
  by.y = "Focus"
)

# -------------------------
# Opportunity Score
# -------------------------

opportunity$OpportunityScore <-
  opportunity$Demand / opportunity$Competition

# Keep top opportunities only
opportunity <- opportunity %>%
  arrange(desc(OpportunityScore)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)

# -------------------------
# Plot
# -------------------------
library(ggrepel)

ggplot(opportunity,
       aes(x = Competition,
           y = Demand,
           size = OpportunityScore,
           color = OpportunityScore,
           label = Service)) +

  # Add slight jitter so points separate
  geom_point(
    position = position_jitter(width = 0.05,
                               height = 0.05),
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +

  # Better label repelling
  geom_text_repel(
    size = 4,
    box.padding = 0.8,
    point.padding = 0.6,
    segment.color = "grey50",
    max.overlaps = Inf
  ) +

  # Quadrant lines
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = mean(opportunity$Competition),
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "red"
  ) +

  geom_hline(
    yintercept = mean(opportunity$Demand),
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "red"
  ) +

  # Expand plot limits for spacing
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0.8, max(opportunity$Competition) + 0.5)
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0.8, max(opportunity$Demand) + 0.5)
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Market Opportunity Matrix",
    subtitle = "High Demand + Low Competition = Best Opportunity",
    x = "Competition Density",
    y = "Market Demand",
    size = "Opportunity",
    color = "Opportunity"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```
